raw_consumer_statement: XX/XX/XXXX I received a text from LLBean Mastercard alert team. It asked if I had made a {$240.00} purchase just then at XXXX. I was just getting ready to call the number on this text when they called me. In addition to the {$240.00} charge had I also purchased {$2000.00} at Best Buy. I assured them I had not and that my card was in my possession. The LLBean rep said they would take these charges off and send me a new card. Upon receiving the new card a few days later I cut up my old card. However a couple weeks later, around XX/XX/XXXX I received an email stating that, since the card used had a chip, I was now responsible for these charges. I called two times to dispute this and was given CitiBank fraud response team number, since this is the card issuer. I then called them and the rep suggested I contact local police. This I did and XXXX Police subsequently was able to get video at both XXXX and Best Buy of the same adult male purchasing these items supposedly with my card. I sent the police report to Citibank Fraud team in XXXX. I then called them XX/XX/XXXX to check on the case and was told they would reopen my case considering the new police information. However, having heard nothing since, I called Citibank fraud today and was told 1 ) case was not reopened and 2 ) police report didnt change anything because a chip card cant be cloned and it was suggested to me that perhaps it was someone I knew who used my card. This is just not true - since the card was not out of my possession. Also I queried why the statement showing the charges put back on my account ended in a number ( XXXX ) that I had never had- not my old card the charges were made on, nor my new LLBean card that was issued to me. I got nowhere and was told I was still responsible for these charges. I am seriously considering getting a lawyer at this point. I did not charge these items, I do not know how this was done - but someone cloned my card or whatever to make these charges. I should not be held responsible for these charges.
